assertions.title: VM guest health checks pass

steps.action: SSH to Node B and verify services are running
steps.expected: All services active (auth, proxy, gateway, vmctl, sandbox)
steps.observed: All 5 services confirmed active via systemctl

steps.action: Call internal vmctl resolve API to trigger VM boot
steps.expected: VM created and boots successfully with Firecracker
steps.observed: VM ownership created (vm-37e40a561d218dcca62e5854c4d2ae63) but Firecracker process exits immediately
No firecracker process found - process exited during boot

steps.action: Check VM guest health endpoint at localhost:9000
steps.observed: Connection refused - no process listening on port 9000

steps.action: Analyze journalctl logs for boot failure root cause
steps.expected: Kernel boots successfully, init script runs, sandbox starts
steps.observed: Kernel panic during boot - cannot mount root filesystem

evidence.network: VM created with sandbox_url=http://127.0.0.1:9000 but never becomes reachable

kernelPanic.error: VFS: Unable to mount root fs on unknown-block(0,0)
kernelPanic.bootArgs: console=ttyS0 reboot=k panic=1 pci=off root=/dev/vda rw init=/bin/init guest_port=8085 persistent=/mnt/persistent vm_id=vm-37e40a561d218dcca62e5854c4d2ae63 epoch=1 ip=172.1.0.2::172.1.0.1:255.255.255.252::eth0:off pci=off root=/dev/vda rw virtio_mmio.device=4K@0xc0001000:6 virtio_mmio.device=4K@0xc0002000:7
kernelPanic.rootCause: Kernel has VIRTIO_MMIO=y but missing VIRTIO_MMIO_CMDLINE_DEVICES=y - virtio-mmio devices specified via command line are not recognized, so /dev/vda block device is never created

assertions.issues: VM guest health checks FAIL. The Firecracker VM boots but kernel panics because it cannot mount the root filesystem. Root cause: kernel config is missing VIRTIO_MMIO_CMDLINE_DEVICES=y which is required for Firecracker's virtio-mmio block device to be recognized when specified via kernel command line. The kernel sees virtio_mmio.device= parameters but doesn't create the corresponding /dev/vda device.

assertions.title: End-to-end provider flow through gateway

steps.expected: Gateway reports healthy with Z.AI provider
steps.observed: Gateway healthy - 13 active identities, rate limiting configured

steps.action: Attempt end-to-end flow via VM-routed path
steps.expected: Request flows: user -> proxy -> VM guest sandbox -> gateway -> Z.AI provider
steps.observed: Cannot complete - VM guest never becomes healthy, no sandbox to route to

evidence.network: Gateway healthy at port 8084, but VM routing path blocked by guest boot failure

assertions.issues: End-to-end provider flow blocked by VAL-VM-004 failure. Gateway is healthy and ready to route to Z.AI provider, but VM guest sandbox cannot start due to kernel panic. The infrastructure for VM routing is in place but the guest VM cannot boot successfully.

assertions.title: Guest VM isolation (guest cannot reach host control-plane)

steps.action: Verify VM boots and becomes reachable
steps.expected: VM guest running at 172.1.0.2 with sandbox responding
steps.observed: VM does not boot - kernel panic prevents guest from starting

steps.action: Test guest isolation from host control-plane
steps.expected: Guest cannot access host services outside allowed ports
steps.observed: Cannot test - no running guest VM to verify isolation

evidence.network: Network configuration prepared (172.1.0.1/30 host, 172.1.0.2/30 guest) but never activated due to boot failure
evidence.iptables: Rules would be configured: MASQUERADE, DNAT for gateway:8084, FORWARD for tap device

assertions.issues: Guest isolation cannot be verified because the guest VM fails to boot. The iptables rules and network isolation infrastructure are in place in the vmmanager code, but the kernel panic prevents the guest from ever starting. Once the kernel config is fixed, this assertion can be re-tested.

assertions.title: Provider secrets absent from guest VM

steps.observed: VM does not boot - kernel panic on root mount

steps.action: Inspect guest environment and filesystem for secrets
steps.expected: No provider credentials in guest env, files, or process args
steps.observed: Cannot inspect - guest never starts, no shell access possible

steps.expected: Provider credentials stored only on host
steps.observed: Confirmed: /var/lib/go-choir/gateway-provider.env exists on host only (not accessible to guest)

hostSecrets.gatewayTokenDelivery: vmctl issues gateway token per-VM and writes to <state-dir>/<vmID>/persist/gateway-token (not provider credentials)

guestDesign.bootArgs: guest_port, vm_id, epoch, ip - NO secrets in kernel cmdline
guestDesign.initScript: reads gateway-token from persistent dir (sandbox auth only, not provider creds)
guestDesign.rootfsContents: sandbox binary, init script, networking tools - no auth/gateway/proxy binaries

assertions.issues: Cannot verify secrets are absent from guest because the guest VM fails to boot. However, the guest image design (in nix/guest-image.nix) explicitly excludes provider credentials and only includes the sandbox runtime. The gateway token issued per-VM is for sandbox-to-gateway authentication only, not provider credentials. Once VM boots successfully, this can be verified by SSH/exec into guest.

frictions.description: Kernel panic debugging required analyzing journalctl logs - the error 'unknown-block(0,0)' is cryptic and required kernel config knowledge to diagnose
frictions.resolution: Identified missing VIRTIO_MMIO_CMDLINE_DEVICES kernel config option

blockers.description: Kernel config missing VIRTIO_MMIO_CMDLINE_DEVICES=y. Firecracker specifies virtio-mmio block device via kernel command line (virtio_mmio.device=4K@0xc0001000:6), but without this config option, the kernel doesn't recognize it and /dev/vda is never created. The kernel has VIRTIO_MMIO=y and VIRTIO_BLK=y, but VIRTIO_MMIO_CMDLINE_DEVICES is required for command-line device specification.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Verified all other kernel configs are correct: EXT4_FS=y, IP_PNP=y, VIRTIO_MMIO=y, VIRTIO_BLK=y, VIRTIO_NET=y. The only missing piece is VIRTIO_MMIO_CMDLINE_DEVICES=y to enable parsing of virtio_mmio.device= parameters.

summary: Tested 4 assertions after Node B redeploy with commit 214b499: 0 passed, 1 failed, 3 blocked. The 'Fix Firecracker guest health and networking' commit addressed many issues (init script, IP_PNP, networking utilities, iptables rules, gateway token flow), but the kernel is still missing VIRTIO_MMIO_CMDLINE_DEVICES=y which prevents the virtio-mmio block device from being recognized. The kernel panics with 'VFS: Unable to mount root fs on unknown-block(0,0)' because /dev/vda is never created. Once this kernel config is added and the guest image is rebuilt, all 4 assertions should be re-testable.

rootCauseAnalysis.issue: Firecracker VM guest kernel panics during boot - cannot mount root filesystem
symptoms: VM ownership created successfully in registry
Firecracker process starts but exits immediately
Kernel panic: VFS: Unable to mount root fs on unknown-block(0,0)
Health check connection refused on guest ports (9000, 9001, etc.)

kernelConfigGap.whyItMatters: Firecracker uses kernel command line to specify virtio-mmio device addresses (virtio_mmio.device=4K@0xc0001000:6). Without VIRTIO_MMIO_CMDLINE_DEVICES, the kernel ignores these parameters and doesn't instantiate the block device.

rootCauseAnalysis.fixRequired: Add VIRTIO_MMIO_CMDLINE_DEVICES = yes to nix/guest-image.nix kernel config, rebuild guest image, redeploy to Node B

recommendations: Add VIRTIO_MMIO_CMDLINE_DEVICES=yes to the kernel structuredExtraConfig in nix/guest-image.nix
Rebuild the guest image with 'nix build .#go-choir-guest-image'
Copy new vmlinux and rootfs.ext4 to /var/lib/go-choir/guest/ on Node B
Re-run this test flow to verify VM boots successfully
